mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing and utility output, which provides immediate positive momentum to equity markets and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal produces negative mean returns in >60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime yields positive forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually confirms robust economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the signal shows negative predictive accuracy in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor demand and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases do not precede positive 60-day returns, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production falls while retail sales rise signals supply chain bottlenecks and inventory depletion, which raises input costs and compresses margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative returns, or forward returns remain positive in >60% of divergence instances during late-cycle phases.
